perturbation.original_text: 6.3 The Buyer acknowledges and agrees that Aldes is, will be an d will remain entitled to receipt of its agreed brokerage commission (albeit due and payable by the Seller/s, the Business/es, the Buyer or any of them ), should the Buyer (albeit directly itself or indirectly t hrough any of its subsidiaries , holding c ompan ies, group c ompanies , related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly) or anyone else the Buyer may have introduced to the Business/es and/or Seller/s , conclude any agreements and/or transactions contemplated in clause s 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Business/es and/or Seller/s .
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 The Buyer acknowledges and agrees that Aldes is entitled to its brokerage commission if the Buyer concludes agreements and/or transactions with any Business/es and/or Seller/s.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a more detailed explanation of Aldes' entitlement to its brokerage commission, specifically addressing scenarios where the Buyer indirectly concludes agreements through subsidiaries or introduces the Business/es to third parties who then conclude agreements. Omitting this specificity creates an in-text contradiction with section 6.2. Section 6.2 states: 'The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negot iating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.' While 6.2 prevents circumvention by the Buyer or its affiliates, the narrower definition of commission entitlement in 6.3 makes it ambiguous whether Aldes is still entitled to commission if, for example, the Buyer's subsidiary enters into an agreement without the Buyer's direct involvement. This ambiguity creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negot iating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the detailed explanation of Aldes' entitlement to commission, specifically regarding scenarios where the Buyer indirectly concludes agreements through subsidiaries. This omission contradicts section 6.2, which guarantees that the Buyer will never circumvent Aldes, directly or indirectly. By narrowing the definition of commission entitlement in 6.3, the changed text creates ambiguity about whether Aldes is entitled to commission if the Buyer's subsidiary enters into an agreement without the Buyer's direct involvement. This ambiguity creates a strong contradiction, uncertainty and potential unenforceability because it is inconsistent with the broad non-circumvention clause in 6.2. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The Buyer acknowledges that Aldes introduces businesses for sale. The Buyer warrants that the information provided in the Buyer’s Profile is accurate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.' and only 'The Buyer acknowledges that Aldes introduces businesses for sale. The Buyer warrants that the information provided in the Buyer’s Profile is accurate.', the modified text removes the ongoing obligation for the Buyer to update Aldes about any changes in their information. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 8.1 itself, since in that section mentions that the Buyer 'will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in the information provided/presented', hence there is no mention now on that same section
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The Buyer acknowledges that Aldes introduces businesses for sale. The Buyer warrants that the information provided in the Buyer’s Profile is accurate.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the buyer must keep Aldes informed of any changes to the information in their profile. The changed text removes this obligation, but the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, does not mention the obligation to keep Aldes updated. While the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the removal of the original obligation in the changed text creates a contradiction. There is now nothing compelling the Buyer to keep their profile information accurate, thus leading to a strong in-text contradiction and uncertainty of each party's obligations and rights.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 Aldes may from time to time, depend ant on the nature, scope and extent of information to be provided to the Buyer, require that the Buyer enter into and conclude further and/or additional confidentiality and non -disclosure agreements. Should the Buyer fail and/or refuse to enter into and co nclude such further agreements, Aldes may, in its sole and absolute discretion, refuse to disclose further information to the Buyer regarding any and/or all Seller/s and/or Business/es.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 Aldes may require the Buyer to enter into additional confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.explanation: By removing ', depend ant on the nature, scope and extent of information to be provided to the Buyer, require that the Buyer enter into and conclude further and/or additional confidentiality and non -disclosure agreements. Should the Buyer fail and/or refuse to enter into and co nclude such further agreements, Aldes may, in its sole and absolute discretion, refuse to disclose further information to the Buyer regarding any and/or all Seller/s and/or Business/es.' and only 'Aldes may require the Buyer to enter into additional confidentiality agreements.' the modified section does not specify the consequence of the Buyer refusing to enter into such agreements. The original section explicitly states that Aldes may refuse to disclose further information, providing Aldes with recourse. The omission of this consequence introduces an in-text contradiction. The modified text implies an obligation on the Buyer to enter into further agreements, but it is unclear what happens if they don't, weakening Aldes' ability to enforce this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Aldes may require the Buyer to enter into additional confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, '9.1 Aldes may require the Buyer to enter into additional confidentiality agreements,' omits the original text's consequence of the Buyer refusing to enter such agreements, specifically that Aldes may refuse to disclose further information. This omission introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now lacks the explicit recourse for Aldes if the Buyer refuses to enter into additional confidentiality agreements. The modified text weakens Aldes' ability to enforce this section, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.6 This Agreement constitutes a stipulatio alteri (namely, a benefit in favor of a third party which may be accepted by such third party) in favor of the Business/es and the Seller/s, the benefits of which may be accepted by them at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and interpreted in accordance with the law of the Republic of South Africa.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the benefit for the Business/es and the Seller/s, which may create an internal contradiction. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 4.1, which says: 'The Buyer undertakes , towards and in favour of each of Aldes, the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) , with regard to and in respect of any and all of the Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials, that it will keep same (irrespective of how same was disclosed to the Buyer ) strictly confidential and secret and that it will hold same in absolute trust and confidence .' Since the Businesses/es and the Seller/s are not part of the stipulatio alteri agreement, their rights are weakend or undefined to take action in case the contract is breached, since section 4.1 refers to this part of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 The Buyer undertakes , towards and in favour of each of Aldes, the Business/es and the Seller/s (jointly and severally) , with regard to and in respect of any and all of the Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials, that it will keep same (irrespective of how same was disclosed to the Buyer ) strictly confidential and secret and that it will hold same in absolute trust and confidence .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly created a stipulatio alteri (third-party benefit) for the Businesses/es and the Seller/s. The changed text removes this benefit and replaces it with a governing law clause. The contradicted text (section 4.1) establishes obligations owed to the Businesses/es and the Seller/s. By removing the stipulatio alteri, the legal basis for the Businesses/es and the Seller/s to enforce these obligations is weakened or eliminated, creating a direct contradiction and impacting the enforceability of section 4.1 as it pertains to those parties. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction', where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction.
